1|# OpenCTI — Cyber Threat Intelligence Platform
  2|# Raw K8s deployment for slim config on 16GB Mac Mini
  3|---
  4|apiVersion: v1
  5|kind: Service
  6|metadata:
  7|  name: opencti
  8|  namespace: opencti
  9|  labels:
 10|    app.kubernetes.io/name: opencti
 11|    app.kubernetes.io/part-of: matchbox-soc
 12|spec:
 13|  selector:
 14|    app.kubernetes.io/name: opencti
 15|  ports:
 16|    - name: http
 17|      port: 4000
 18|      targetPort: 4000
 19|---
 20|apiVersion: apps/v1
 21|kind: Deployment
 22|metadata:
 23|  name: opencti
 24|  namespace: opencti
 25|  labels:
 26|    app.kubernetes.io/name: opencti
 27|    app.kubernetes.io/part-of: matchbox-soc
 28|spec:
 29|  replicas: 1
 30|  selector:
 31|    matchLabels:
 32|      app.kubernetes.io/name: opencti
 33|  template:
 34|    metadata:
 35|      labels:
 36|        app.kubernetes.io/name: opencti
 37|    spec:
 38|      automountServiceAccountToken: false
 39|      securityContext:
 40|        runAsNonRoot: true
 41|        runAsUser: 1000
 42|        fsGroup: 1000
 43|      containers:
 44|        - name: opencti
 45|          image: opencti/platform:6.6.6
 46|          securityContext:
 47|            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
 48|            readOnlyRootFilesystem: false
 49|            capabilities:
 50|              drop: ["ALL"]
 51|          ports:
 52|            - containerPort: 4000
 53|              name: http
 54|          resources:
 55|            requests:
 56|              memory: "1Gi"
 57|              cpu: "250m"
 58|            limits:
 59|              memory: "1536Mi"
 60|              cpu: "500m"
 61|          env:
 62|            - name: NODE_OPTIONS
 63|              value: "--max-old-space-size=1024"
 64|            - name: APP__PORT
 65|              value: "4000"
 66|            - name: APP__BASE_URL
 67|              value: "http://opencti.soc.homelab.local"
 68|            - name: APP__ADMIN__EMAIL
 69|              value: "admin@soc.local"
 70|            - name: APP__ADMIN__PASSWORD
 71|              valueFrom:
 72|                secretKeyRef:
 73|                  name: soc-opencti-secrets
 74|                  key: admin-password
 75|            - name: APP__ADMIN__TOKEN
 76|              valueFrom:
 77|                secretKeyRef:
 78|                  name: soc-opencti-secrets
 79|                  key: admin-token
 80|            - name: APP__HEALTH_ACCESS_KEY
 81|              valueFrom:
 82|                secretKeyRef:
 83|                  name: soc-opencti-secrets
 84|                  key: health-access-key
 85|            # OpenSearch (HTTPS with self-signed cert)
 86|            - name: ELASTICSEARCH__URL
 87|              value: "https://opensearch-cluster-master.shared.svc.cluster.

88|            - name: ELASTICSEARCH__USERNAME
 89|              value: "admin"
 90|            - name: ELASTICSEARCH__PASSWORD
 91|              valueFrom:
 92|                secretKeyRef:
 93|                  name: soc-shared-secrets
 94|                  key: opensearch-password
 95|            - name: ELASTICSEARCH__SSL__REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED
 96|              value: "false"
 97|            - name: NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED
 98|              value: "0"
 99|            # Redis
100|            - name: REDIS__HOSTNAME
101|              value: "redis-master.shared.svc.cluster.local"
102|            - name: REDIS__PORT
103|              value: "6379"
104|            - name: REDIS__PASSWORD
105|              valueFrom:
106|                secretKeyRef:
107|                  name: soc-shared-secrets
108|                  key: redis-password
109|            # RabbitMQ
110|            - name: RABBITMQ__HOSTNAME
111|              value: "rabbitmq.shared.svc.cluster.local"
112|            - name: RABBITMQ__PORT
113|              value: "5672"
114|            - name: RABBITMQ__USERNAME
115|              value: "opencti"
116|            - name: RABBITMQ__PASSWORD
117|              valueFrom:
118|                secretKeyRef:
119|                  name: soc-shared-secrets
120|                  key: rabbitmq-password
121|            # MinIO
122|            - name: MINIO__ENDPOINT
123|              value: "minio.shared.svc.cluster.local"
124|            - name: MINIO__PORT
125|              value: "9000"
126|            - name: MINIO__USE_SSL
127|              value: "false"
128|            - name: MINIO__ACCESS_KEY
129|              valueFrom:
130|                secretKeyRef:
131|                  name: soc-shared-secrets
132|                  key: minio-root-user
133|            - name: MINIO__SECRET_KEY
134|              valueFrom:
135|                secretKeyRef:
136|                  name: soc-shared-secrets
137|                  key: minio-root-password
138|            - name: MINIO__BUCKET_NAME
139|              value: "opencti-bucket"
140|            - name: PROVIDERS__LOCAL__STRATEGY
141|              value: "LocalStrategy"
142|          readinessProbe:
143|            httpGet:
144|              port: 4000
145|              path: /health
146|            initialDelaySeconds: 60
147|            periodSeconds: 15
148|          livenessProbe:
149|            httpGet:
150|              port: 4000
151|              path: /health
152|            initialDelaySeconds: 120
153|            periodSeconds: 30
154|---
155|# OpenCTI Worker — processes ingestion tasks from RabbitMQ
156|apiVersion: apps/v1
157|kind: Deployment
158|metadata:
159|  name: opencti-worker
160|  namespace: opencti
161|  labels:
162|    app.kubernetes.io/name: opencti-worker
163|    app.kubernetes.io/part-of: matchbox-soc
164|spec:
165|  replicas: 1
166|  selector:
167|    matchLabels:
168|      app.kubernetes.io/name: opencti-worker
169|  template:
170|    metadata:
171|      labels:
172|        app.kubernetes.io/name: opencti-worker
173|    spec:
174|      automountServiceAccountToken: false
175|      securityContext:
176|        runAsNonRoot: true
177|        runAsUser: 1000
178|        fsGroup: 1000
179|      containers:
180|        - name: worker
181|          image: opencti/worker:6.6.

6.6
182|          securityContext:
183|            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
184|            readOnlyRootFilesystem: false
185|            capabilities:
186|              drop: ["ALL"]
187|          resources:
188|            requests:
189|              memory: "256Mi"
190|              cpu: "100m"
191|            limits:
192|              memory: "512Mi"
193|              cpu: "250m"
194|          env:
195|            - name: OPENCTI_URL
196|              value: "http://opencti.opencti.svc.cluster.local:4000"
197|            - name: OPENCTI_TOKEN
198|              valueFrom:
199|                secretKeyRef:
200|                  name: soc-opencti-secrets
201|                  key: admin-token
202|            - name: WORKER_LOG_LEVEL
203|              value: "info"
204|

1|# OpenCTI Helm Values — Cyber Threat Intelligence Platform
 2|# Chart: opencti/opencti (https://devops-ia.github.io/helm-opencti/)
 3|#
 4|# Install:
 5|#   helm repo add opencti https://devops-ia.github.io/helm-opencti/
 6|#   helm install opencti opencti/opencti -n opencti -f helm-values.yaml
 7|#
 8|# OpenCTI is the most resource-hungry component. On 16GB Mac Mini, we run
 9|# slim mode: 1 worker, connectors on-demand, strict memory limits.
10|
11|# --- OpenCTI Platform ---
12|opencti:
13|  replicaCount: 1
14|  resources:
15|    requests:
16|      memory: "1Gi"
17|      cpu: "250m"
18|    limits:
19|      memory: "1536Mi"  # 1.5GB limit
20|      cpu: "500m"
21|
22|  # Application settings
23|  env:
24|    # Admin credentials — password from K8s Secret
25|    OPENCTI_ADMIN_EMAIL: "admin@soc.local"
26|    OPENCTI_ADMIN_TOKEN: ""  # Auto-generated, retrieve after first boot
27|
28|    # Shared infrastructure connections
29|    ELASTICSEARCH__URL: "http://opensearch.shared.svc.cluster.local:9200"
30|    REDIS__HOSTNAME: "redis-master.shared.svc.cluster.local"
31|    REDIS__PORT: "6379"
32|    RABBITMQ__HOSTNAME: "rabbitmq.shared.svc.cluster.local"
33|    RABBITMQ__PORT: "5672"
34|    RABBITMQ__USERNAME: "opencti"
35|    MINIO__ENDPOINT: "minio.shared.svc.cluster.local"
36|    MINIO__PORT: "9000"
37|    MINIO__USE_SSL: "false"
38|    MINIO__ACCESS_KEY: ""  # Overridden by soc-shared-secrets/minio-root-user via extraEnvs
39|    MINIO__BUCKET_NAME: "opencti-bucket"
40|
41|    # Performance tuning for slim config
42|    PROVIDERS__LOCAL__STRATEGY: "LocalStrategy"
43|
44|  # Credentials from K8s Secrets (never hardcoded)
45|  extraEnvs:
46|    - name: OPENCTI_ADMIN_PASSWORD
47|      valueFrom:
48|        secretKeyRef:
49|          name: soc-opencti-secrets
50|          key: admin-password
51|    - name: RABBITMQ__PASSWORD
52|      valueFrom:
53|        secretKeyRef:
54|          name: soc-shared-secrets
55|          key: rabbitmq-password
56|    - name: MINIO__SECRET_KEY
57|      valueFrom:
58|        secretKeyRef:
59|          name: soc-shared-secrets
60|          key: minio-root-password
61|    - name: APP__HEALTH_ACCESS_KEY
62|      valueFrom:
63|        secretKeyRef:
64|          name: soc-opencti-secrets
65|          key: health-access-key
66|
67|# --- Worker ---
68|# Workers process ingestion tasks from the RabbitMQ queue
69|# On slim config, run only 1 worker (default is 3)
70|worker:
71|  replicaCount: 1
72|  resources:
73|    requests:
74|      memory: "256Mi"
75|      cpu: "100m"
76|    limits:
77|      memory: "512Mi"
78|      cpu: "250m"
79|
80|# --- Service ---
81|service:
82|  type: ClusterIP
83|  port: 4000
84|
85|# --- Persistence ---
86|persistence:
87|  enabled: true
88|  size: "10Gi"
89|  storageClass: "local-path"
90|

1|# MinIO Helm Values — S3-compatible object storage for OpenCTI and TheHive
 2|# Chart: minio/minio (https://charts.min.io/)
 3|#
 4|# Install:
 5|#   helm repo add minio https://charts.min.io/
 6|#   helm install minio minio/minio -n shared -f minio.yaml
 7|
 8|# Single-node mode (standalone)
 9|mode: standalone
10|
11|# Auth — credentials pulled from K8s Secret (see k8s/shared/secrets.yaml)
12|existingSecret: soc-shared-secrets
13|
14|# Resource limits — slim config
15|resources:
16|  requests:
17|    memory: "256Mi"
18|    cpu: "100m"
19|  limits:
20|    memory: "512Mi"
21|    cpu: "250m"
22|
23|# Persistent storage — 40GB for artifacts and threat intel files
24|persistence:
25|  enabled: true
26|  size: "40Gi"
27|  storageClass: "local-path"
28|
29|# Create default buckets on startup
30|buckets:
31|  - name: opencti-bucket
32|    policy: none
33|    purge: false
34|  - name: thehive-bucket
35|    policy: none
36|    purge: false
37|  - name: cortex-bucket
38|    policy: none
39|    purge: false
40|
41|# Disable console (saves RAM, we manage via CLI or API)
42|console:
43|  enabled: false
44|
45|# Metrics — enable for Prometheus
46|metrics:
47|  serviceMonitor:
48|    enabled: true
49|

1|# OpenSearch Helm Values — Shared instance for Wazuh, OpenCTI, TheHive
 2|# Chart: opensearch/opensearch (https://opensearch-project.github.io/helm-charts/)
 3|#
 4|# Why shared? Running separate ES/OpenSearch instances per service would cost
 5|# 6-12GB RAM. A single shared instance with index-per-service isolation
 6|# uses only 2GB. Trade-off: shared failure domain (acceptable for home lab).
 7|#
 8|# Install:
 9|#   helm repo add opensearch https://opensearch-project.github.io/helm-charts/
10|#   helm install opensearch opensearch/opensearch -n shared -f opensearch.yaml
11|
12|# Single-node mode (no replicas — home lab doesn't need HA)
13|singleNode: true
14|replicas: 1
15|
16|# Resource limits — slim config for 16GB Mac Mini
17|resources:
18|  requests:
19|    memory: "1.5Gi"
20|    cpu: "500m"
21|  limits:
22|    memory: "2Gi"
23|    cpu: "1000m"
24|
25|# JVM heap — 1GB heap (half of 2GB container limit, OpenSearch best practice)
26|opensearchJavaOpts: "-Xmx1g -Xms1g"
27|
28|# Persistent storage — 40GB for all indices
29|persistence:
30|  enabled: true
31|  size: "40Gi"
32|  storageClass: "local-path"  # k3s built-in provisioner
33|
34|# Security — enabled with internal users for service-to-service auth
35|securityConfig:
36|  enabled: true
37|  config:
38|    securityConfigSecret: ""  # Uses default internal_users.yml
39|    dataComplete: false
40|
41|# Environment variables — credentials from K8s Secret
42|extraEnvs:
43|  - name: OPENSEARCH_INITIAL_ADMIN_PASSWORD
44|    valueFrom:
45|      secretKeyRef:
46|        name: soc-shared-secrets
47|        key: opensearch-password
48|
49|# Index lifecycle — auto-delete old indices to save disk
50|# Wazuh alerts: 7-day retention
51|# OpenCTI STIX data: 90-day retention
52|# TheHive case data: no auto-delete
53|extraConfig:
54|  opensearch.yml: |
55|    cluster.name: matchbox-soc
56|    node.name: opensearch-0
57|

1|# RabbitMQ — Official image deployment (replaces Bitnami chart)
  2|# Uses rabbitmq:4-management-alpine for OpenCTI async messaging
  3|---
  4|apiVersion: v1
  5|kind: Service
  6|metadata:
  7|  name: rabbitmq
  8|  namespace: shared
  9|  labels:
 10|    app.kubernetes.io/name: rabbitmq
 11|    app.kubernetes.io/part-of: matchbox-soc
 12|spec:
 13|  selector:
 14|    app.kubernetes.io/name: rabbitmq
 15|  ports:
 16|    - name: amqp
 17|      port: 5672
 18|      targetPort: 5672
 19|    - name: management
 20|      port: 15672
 21|      targetPort: 15672
 22|    - name: prometheus
 23|      port: 15692
 24|      targetPort: 15692
 25|  clusterIP: None
 26|---
 27|apiVersion: apps/v1
 28|kind: StatefulSet
 29|metadata:
 30|  name: rabbitmq
 31|  namespace: shared
 32|  labels:
 33|    app.kubernetes.io/name: rabbitmq
 34|    app.kubernetes.io/part-of: matchbox-soc
 35|spec:
 36|  serviceName: rabbitmq
 37|  replicas: 1
 38|  selector:
 39|    matchLabels:
 40|      app.kubernetes.io/name: rabbitmq
 41|  template:
 42|    metadata:
 43|      labels:
 44|        app.kubernetes.io/name: rabbitmq
 45|    spec:
 46|      automountServiceAccountToken: false
 47|      securityContext:
 48|        runAsNonRoot: true
 49|        runAsUser: 1000
 50|        fsGroup: 1000
 51|      containers:
 52|        - name: rabbitmq
 53|          image: rabbitmq:4.1-management-alpine
 54|          securityContext:
 55|            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
 56|            readOnlyRootFilesystem: false
 57|            capabilities:
 58|              drop: ["ALL"]
 59|          ports:
 60|            - containerPort: 5672
 61|              name: amqp
 62|            - containerPort: 15672
 63|              name: management
 64|            - containerPort: 15692
 65|              name: prometheus
 66|          env:
 67|            - name: RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_USER
 68|              value: "opencti"
 69|            - name: RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_PASS
 70|              valueFrom:
 71|                secretKeyRef:
 72|                  name: soc-shared-secrets
 73|                  key: rabbitmq-password
 74|          resources:
 75|            requests:
 76|              memory: "128Mi"
 77|              cpu: "50m"
 78|            limits:
 79|              memory: "256Mi"
 80|              cpu: "200m"
 81|          readinessProbe:
 82|            tcpSocket:
 83|              port: 5672
 84|            initialDelaySeconds: 15
 85|            periodSeconds: 10
 86|            timeoutSeconds: 5
 87|          livenessProbe:
 88|            tcpSocket:
 89|              port: 5672
 90|            initialDelaySeconds: 45
 91|            periodSeconds: 30
 92|            timeoutSeconds: 5
 93|            failureThreshold: 6
 94|          volumeMounts:
 95|            - name: data
 96|              mountPath: /var/lib/rabbitmq
 97|            - name: config
 98|              mountPath: /etc/rabbitmq/conf.d/20-matchbox.conf
 99|              subPath: rabbitmq.

99|              subPath: rabbitmq.conf
100|      volumes:
101|        - name: config
102|          configMap:
103|            name: rabbitmq-config
104|  volumeClaimTemplates:
105|    - metadata:
106|        name: data
107|      spec:
108|        accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
109|        storageClassName: "local-path"
110|        resources:
111|          requests:
112|            storage: 1Gi
113|

1|# RabbitMQ Helm Values — Used by OpenCTI for async message processing
 2|# Chart: bitnami/rabbitmq (https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami)
 3|#
 4|# Install:
 5|#   helm install rabbitmq bitnami/rabbitmq -n shared -f rabbitmq.yaml
 6|
 7|# Single replica — home lab doesn't need clustering
 8|replicaCount: 1
 9|
10|# Auth — credentials pulled from K8s Secret (see k8s/shared/secrets.yaml)
11|auth:
12|  username: opencti
13|  existingPasswordSecret: soc-shared-secrets
14|  existingSecretPasswordKey: rabbitmq-password
15|  existingErlangSecret: soc-shared-secrets
16|  existingSecretErlangKey: rabbitmq-erlang-cookie
17|
18|# Resource limits — slim config
19|resources:
20|  requests:
21|    memory: "128Mi"
22|    cpu: "50m"
23|  limits:
24|    memory: "256Mi"
25|    cpu: "100m"
26|
27|# Persistent storage
28|persistence:
29|  enabled: true
30|  size: "1Gi"
31|  storageClass: "local-path"
32|
33|# Management plugin — enable web UI for debugging (internal only)
34|plugins: "rabbitmq_management rabbitmq_peer_discovery_k8s"
35|
36|# Metrics — enable for Prometheus
37|metrics:
38|  enabled: true
39|  resources:
40|    requests:
41|      memory: "32Mi"
42|      cpu: "10m"
43|    limits:
44|      memory: "64Mi"
45|      cpu: "50m"
46|
47|# Memory limit — tell RabbitMQ to use at most 200MB before raising alarms
48|# This prevents it from eating into our tight RAM budget
49|communityPlugins: ""
50|extraConfiguration: |
51|  vm_memory_high_watermark.absolute = 200MB
52|  disk_free_limit.absolute = 500MB
53|

1|# Redis Helm Values — Used by OpenCTI for caching and streaming
 2|# Chart: bitnami/redis (https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami)
 3|#
 4|# Install:
 5|#   helm repo add bitnami https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
 6|#   helm install redis bitnami/redis -n shared -f redis.yaml
 7|
 8|# Standalone mode (no sentinel, no cluster — overkill for home lab)
 9|architecture: standalone
10|
11|# Auth — enabled with password from K8s Secret (defense in depth)
12|auth:
13|  enabled: true
14|  existingSecret: soc-shared-secrets
15|  existingSecretPasswordKey: redis-password
16|
17|# Resource limits — slim config
18|master:
19|  resources:
20|    requests:
21|      memory: "128Mi"
22|      cpu: "50m"
23|    limits:
24|      memory: "256Mi"
25|      cpu: "100m"
26|  persistence:
27|    enabled: true
28|    size: "1Gi"
29|    storageClass: "local-path"
30|
31|# Disable replicas (standalone mode)
32|replica:
33|  replicaCount: 0
34|
35|# Metrics — enable for Prometheus scraping
36|metrics:
37|  enabled: true
38|  resources:
39|    requests:
40|      memory: "32Mi"
41|      cpu: "10m"
42|    limits:
43|      memory: "64Mi"
44|      cpu: "50m"
45|

1|# NFS Server for Cortex Shared Filesystem
 2|# Cortex requires a shared filesystem accessible by both the Cortex pod
 3|# and the analyzer/responder Job pods it creates.
 4|# Without this, analyzer jobs fail silently because they can't read input
 5|# or write output.
 6|#
 7|# This deploys a lightweight NFS server (nfs-ganesha) as a pod in the
 8|# shared namespace, with a PersistentVolume backing it.
 9|---
10|apiVersion: v1
11|kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
12|metadata:
13|  name: nfs-data
14|  namespace: shared
15|spec:
16|  accessModes:
17|    - ReadWriteOnce
18|  storageClassName: "local-path"
19|  resources:
20|    requests:
21|      storage: "5Gi"
22|---
23|apiVersion: apps/v1
24|kind: Deployment
25|metadata:
26|  name: nfs-server
27|  namespace: shared
28|  labels:
29|    app: nfs-server
30|spec:
31|  replicas: 1
32|  selector:
33|    matchLabels:
34|      app: nfs-server
35|  template:
36|    metadata:
37|      labels:
38|        app: nfs-server
39|    spec:
40|      containers:
41|        - name: nfs-ganesha
42|          image: erichough/nfs-server:2.2.1
43|          resources:
44|            requests:
45|              memory: "64Mi"
46|              cpu: "50m"
47|            limits:
48|              memory: "128Mi"
49|              cpu: "100m"
50|          ports:
51|            - containerPort: 2049
52|              name: nfs
53|              protocol: TCP
54|          env:
55|            - name: NFS_EXPORT_0
56|              value: "/exports/cortex *(rw,sync,no_subtree_check,root_squash)"
57|          volumeMounts:
58|            - name: nfs-data
59|              mountPath: /exports/cortex
60|          securityContext:
61|            privileged: true  # Required for NFS server
62|      volumes:
63|        - name: nfs-data
64|          persistentVolumeClaim:
65|            claimName: nfs-data
66|---
67|apiVersion: v1
68|kind: Service
69|metadata:
70|  name: nfs-server
71|  namespace: shared
72|  labels:
73|    app: nfs-server
74|spec:
75|  selector:
76|    app: nfs-server
77|  ports:
78|    - port: 2049
79|      targetPort: 2049
80|      protocol: TCP
81|  type: ClusterIP
82|

1|# Shared PersistentVolumeClaims
 2|# These PVCs use k3s local-path-provisioner for automatic volume provisioning
 3|# on the single-node cluster. In a multi-node setup, replace with NFS or
 4|# a distributed storage class.
 5|---
 6|# Cortex shared data — NFS-backed PV for analyzer job I/O
 7|apiVersion: v1
 8|kind: PersistentVolume
 9|metadata:
10|  name: cortex-shared-nfs
11|spec:
12|  capacity:
13|    storage: 2Gi
14|  accessModes:
15|    - ReadWriteMany  # Multiple pods (Cortex + analyzer jobs) need simultaneous access
16|  nfs:
17|    server: nfs-server.shared.svc.cluster.local
18|    path: "/exports/cortex"
19|  persistentVolumeReclaimPolicy: Retain
20|---
21|apiVersion: v1
22|kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
23|metadata:
24|  name: cortex-shared-data
25|  namespace: thehive
26|spec:
27|  accessModes:
28|    - ReadWriteMany
29|  storageClassName: ""  # Use pre-provisioned NFS PV
30|  volumeName: cortex-shared-nfs
31|  resources:
32|    requests:
33|      storage: 2Gi
34|

1|# Cortex — Automated Analysis Engine for TheHive
 2|# Runs analyzers (VirusTotal, AbuseIPDB, GeoIP) as K8s Jobs
 3|---
 4|apiVersion: v1
 5|kind: Service
 6|metadata:
 7|  name: cortex
 8|  namespace: thehive
 9|  labels:
10|    app.kubernetes.io/name: cortex
11|    app.kubernetes.io/part-of: matchbox-soc
12|spec:
13|  selector:
14|    app.kubernetes.io/name: cortex
15|  ports:
16|    - name: http
17|      port: 9001
18|      targetPort: 9001
19|---
20|apiVersion: apps/v1
21|kind: StatefulSet
22|metadata:
23|  name: cortex
24|  namespace: thehive
25|  labels:
26|    app.kubernetes.io/name: cortex
27|    app.kubernetes.io/part-of: matchbox-soc
28|spec:
29|  serviceName: cortex
30|  replicas: 1
31|  selector:
32|    matchLabels:
33|      app.kubernetes.io/name: cortex
34|  template:
35|    metadata:
36|      labels:
37|        app.kubernetes.io/name: cortex
38|    spec:
39|      serviceAccountName: cortex-job-runner
40|      securityContext:
41|        runAsNonRoot: true
42|        runAsUser: 1000
43|        fsGroup: 1000
44|      containers:
45|        - name: cortex
46|          image: thehiveproject/cortex:3.1.8
47|          securityContext:
48|            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
49|            readOnlyRootFilesystem: false
50|            capabilities:
51|              drop: ["ALL"]
52|          ports:
53|            - containerPort: 9001
54|              name: http
55|          resources:
56|            requests:
57|              memory: "256Mi"
58|              cpu: "100m"
59|            limits:
60|              memory: "512Mi"
61|              cpu: "250m"
62|          env:
63|            - name: es_uri
64|              value: "http://opensearch-cluster-master.shared.svc.cluster.local:9200"
65|            - name: es_index
66|              value: "cortex"
67|            - name: job_runner
68|              value: "kubernetes"
69|            - name: job_kubernetes_namespace
70|              value: "thehive"
71|            - name: job_kubernetes_service_account
72|              value: "cortex-job-runner"
73|          volumeMounts:
74|            - name: data
75|              mountPath: /opt/cortex/data
76|          readinessProbe:
77|            httpGet:
78|              port: 9001
79|              path: /api/status
80|            initialDelaySeconds: 30
81|            periodSeconds: 15
82|          livenessProbe:
83|            httpGet:
84|              port: 9001
85|              path: /api/status
86|            initialDelaySeconds: 60
87|            periodSeconds: 30
88|  volumeClaimTemplates:
89|    - metadata:
90|        name: data
91|      spec:
92|        accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
93|        storageClassName: "local-path"
94|        resources:
95|          requests:
96|            storage: 2Gi
97|

1|# Cortex Helm Values — Automated Analysis Engine
 2|# Chart: strangebee/cortex (https://strangebee.github.io/helm-charts/)
 3|#
 4|# Install:
 5|#   helm install cortex strangebee/cortex -n thehive -f cortex/helm-values.yaml
 6|
 7|cortex:
 8|  # Resource limits — slim config
 9|  resources:
10|    requests:
11|      memory: "256Mi"
12|      cpu: "100m"
13|    limits:
14|      memory: "512Mi"
15|      cpu: "250m"
16|
17|  # Cortex uses its own Elasticsearch index (separate from TheHive)
18|  elasticsearch:
19|    url: "http://opensearch.shared.svc.cluster.local:9200"
20|    indexPrefix: "cortex"
21|
22|  # Kubernetes job runner — how Cortex executes analyzers
23|  job:
24|    # Cortex creates K8s Jobs for each analyzer/responder invocation
25|    runner: "kubernetes"
26|    kubernetes:
27|      # Namespace where analyzer jobs run
28|      namespace: "thehive"
29|      # ServiceAccount with permission to create Jobs (see rbac.yaml)
30|      serviceAccount: "cortex-job-runner"
31|      # NFS volume for sharing data between Cortex and analyzer pods
32|      sharedVolume:
33|        nfs:
34|          server: "nfs-server.shared.svc.cluster.local"
35|          path: "/exports/cortex"
36|      # Resource limits for individual analyzer pods
37|      jobResources:
38|        requests:
39|          memory: "128Mi"
40|          cpu: "100m"
41|        limits:
42|          memory: "512Mi"
43|          cpu: "300m"
44|
45|  # Analyzer configuration
46|  analyzers:
47|    # VirusTotal — file/hash/IP/domain reputation
48|    - name: "VirusTotal_GetReport_3_1"
49|      enabled: true
50|      config:
51|        # API key set via Secret
52|        auto_extract_artifacts: true
53|
54|    # AbuseIPDB — IP reputation
55|    - name: "AbuseIPDB_1_0"
56|      enabled: true
57|      config:
58|        max_age_in_days: 30
59|
60|    # URLhaus — malicious URL detection
61|    - name: "URLhaus_2_0"
62|      enabled: true
63|
64|    # MaxMind GeoIP — IP geolocation
65|    - name: "MaxMind_GeoIP_4_0"
66|      enabled: true
67|
68|    # FileInfo — basic file analysis (hash, MIME, size)
69|    - name: "FileInfo_8_0"
70|      enabled: true
71|
72|# Service
73|service:
74|  type: ClusterIP
75|  port: 9001
76|
77|# Persistent storage
78|persistence:
79|  enabled: true
80|  size: "2Gi"
81|  storageClass: "local-path"
82|

1|# Cortex RBAC — ServiceAccount + permissions for creating Kubernetes Jobs
 2|# Cortex needs to create and manage Jobs in the thehive namespace to run analyzers.
 3|# Each analyzer runs as a separate K8s Job (one pod per analysis request).
 4|# Without this RBAC, every Cortex analyzer invocation fails silently.
 5|---
 6|apiVersion: v1
 7|kind: ServiceAccount
 8|metadata:
 9|  name: cortex-job-runner
10|  namespace: thehive
11|  labels:
12|    app: cortex
13|---
14|apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
15|kind: Role
16|metadata:
17|  name: cortex-job-runner
18|  namespace: thehive
19|  labels:
20|    app: cortex
21|rules:
22|  # Create and manage analyzer/responder Jobs
23|  - apiGroups: ["batch"]
24|    resources: ["jobs"]
25|    verbs: ["create", "get", "list", "watch", "delete"]
26|  # Read pod logs (for analyzer output)
27|  - apiGroups: [""]
28|    resources: ["pods", "pods/log"]
29|    verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
30|  # Manage PVCs for analyzer data (NFS claims)
31|  - apiGroups: [""]
32|    resources: ["persistentvolumeclaims"]
33|    verbs: ["create", "get", "list", "patch", "delete"]
34|---
35|apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
36|kind: RoleBinding
37|metadata:
38|  name: cortex-job-runner
39|  namespace: thehive
40|  labels:
41|    app: cortex
42|roleRef:
43|  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
44|  kind: Role
45|  name: cortex-job-runner
46|subjects:
47|  - kind: ServiceAccount
48|    name: cortex-job-runner
49|    namespace: thehive
50|

1|# TheHive 5 — Incident Response Platform
  2|# Raw K8s manifests (StrangeBee doesn't maintain a public Helm chart)
  3|---
  4|apiVersion: v1
  5|kind: Service
  6|metadata:
  7|  name: thehive
  8|  namespace: thehive
  9|  labels:
 10|    app.kubernetes.io/name: thehive
 11|    app.kubernetes.io/part-of: matchbox-soc
 12|spec:
 13|  selector:
 14|    app.kubernetes.io/name: thehive
 15|  ports:
 16|    - name: http
 17|      port: 9000
 18|      targetPort: 9000
 19|  clusterIP: None
 20|---
 21|apiVersion: apps/v1
 22|kind: StatefulSet
 23|metadata:
 24|  name: thehive
 25|  namespace: thehive
 26|  labels:
 27|    app.kubernetes.io/name: thehive
 28|    app.kubernetes.io/part-of: matchbox-soc
 29|spec:
 30|  serviceName: thehive
 31|  replicas: 1
 32|  selector:
 33|    matchLabels:
 34|      app.kubernetes.io/name: thehive
 35|  template:
 36|    metadata:
 37|      labels:
 38|        app.kubernetes.io/name: thehive
 39|    spec:
 40|      automountServiceAccountToken: false
 41|      securityContext:
 42|        runAsNonRoot: true
 43|        runAsUser: 1000
 44|        fsGroup: 1000
 45|      containers:
 46|        - name: thehive
 47|          image: strangebee/thehive:5.4
 48|          securityContext:
 49|            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
 50|            readOnlyRootFilesystem: false
 51|            capabilities:
 52|              drop: ["ALL"]
 53|          ports:
 54|            - containerPort: 9000
 55|              name: http
 56|          resources:
 57|            requests:
 58|              memory: "512Mi"
 59|              cpu: "250m"
 60|            limits:
 61|              memory: "1Gi"
 62|              cpu: "500m"
 63|          env:
 64|            # Database — shared OpenSearch
 65|            - name: TH_DB_PROVIDER
 66|              value: "elasticsearch"
 67|            - name: TH_DB_ES_URI
 68|              value: "https://opensearch-cluster-master.shared.svc.cluster.local:9200"
 69|            - name: TH_DB_ES_SSL_ENABLED
 70|              value: "true"
 71|            - name: TH_DB_ES_SSL_VERIFY
 72|              value: "false"
 73|            - name: TH_DB_ES_INDEX_PREFIX
 74|              value: "thehive"
 75|            # Storage — shared MinIO
 76|            - name: TH_STORAGE_PROVIDER
 77|              value: "s3"
 78|            - name: TH_STORAGE_S3_ENDPOINT
 79|              value: "http://minio.shared.svc.cluster.local:9000"
 80|            - name: TH_STORAGE_S3_BUCKET
 81|              value: "thehive-bucket"
 82|            - name: TH_STORAGE_S3_ACCESS_KEY
 83|              valueFrom:
 84|                secretKeyRef:
 85|                  name: soc-shared-secrets
 86|                  key: minio-root-user
 87|            - name: TH_STORAGE_S3_SECRET_KEY
 88|              valueFrom:
 89|                secretKeyRef:
 90|                  name: soc-shared-secrets
 91|                  key: minio-root-password
 92|            # Cortex integration
 93|            - name: TH_CORTEX_URLS
 94|              value: "http://cortex.thehive.svc.cluster.

95|            # Play framework secret
 96|            - name: TH_SECRET
 97|              valueFrom:
 98|                secretKeyRef:
 99|                  name: soc-thehive-secrets
100|                  key: play-secret
101|          volumeMounts:
102|            - name: data
103|              mountPath: /opt/thp/thehive/data
104|          readinessProbe:
105|            httpGet:
106|              port: 9000
107|              path: /api/v1/status/public
108|            initialDelaySeconds: 30
109|            periodSeconds: 15
110|          livenessProbe:
111|            httpGet:
112|              port: 9000
113|              path: /api/v1/status/public
114|            initialDelaySeconds: 60
115|            periodSeconds: 30
116|  volumeClaimTemplates:
117|    - metadata:
118|        name: data
119|      spec:
120|        accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
121|        storageClassName: "local-path"
122|        resources:
123|          requests:
124|            storage: 5Gi
125|

1|# TheHive Helm Values — Incident Response Platform
 2|# Chart: strangebee/thehive (https://strangebee.github.io/helm-charts/)
 3|#
 4|# Install:
 5|#   helm repo add strangebee https://strangebee.github.io/helm-charts/
 6|#   helm install thehive strangebee/thehive -n thehive -f helm-values.yaml
 7|
 8|# TheHive application config
 9|thehive:
10|  # Resource limits — slim config
11|  resources:
12|    requests:
13|      memory: "512Mi"
14|      cpu: "250m"
15|    limits:
16|      memory: "1Gi"
17|      cpu: "500m"
18|
19|  # Database backend — use shared OpenSearch
20|  database:
21|    provider: elasticsearch
22|    elasticsearch:
23|      url: "http://opensearch.shared.svc.cluster.local:9200"
24|      # TheHive uses its own index prefix to avoid collision with Wazuh/OpenCTI
25|      indexPrefix: "thehive"
26|
27|  # File storage — use shared MinIO (secret key from K8s Secret)
28|  storage:
29|    provider: s3
30|    s3:
31|      endpoint: "http://minio.shared.svc.cluster.local:9000"
32|      bucket: "thehive-bucket"
33|      accessKey: ""  # Overridden by soc-shared-secrets/minio-root-user
34|      secretKeyFrom:
35|        secretKeyRef:
36|          name: soc-shared-secrets
37|          key: minio-root-password
38|
39|  # Cortex integration
40|  cortex:
41|    servers:
42|      - name: "cortex-local"
43|        url: "http://cortex.thehive.svc.cluster.local:9001"
44|        # API key set via Kubernetes Secret
45|        auth:
46|          type: "bearer"
47|
48|  # Application config overrides — Play secret from K8s Secret
49|  application:
50|    secretFrom:
51|      secretKeyRef:
52|        name: soc-thehive-secrets
53|        key: play-secret
54|
55|# Service configuration
56|service:
57|  type: ClusterIP
58|  port: 9000
59|
60|# Persistent storage for TheHive data
61|persistence:
62|  enabled: true
63|  size: "5Gi"
64|  storageClass: "local-path"
65|

1|#!/usr/bin/env python3
  2|"""
  3|Wazuh-to-TheHive Integration Script (custom-w2thive.py)
  4|
  5|This script is called by the Wazuh Manager's active response system when an
  6|alert meets the configured threshold (level 7+). It creates a TheHive alert
  7|with extracted observables for analyst triage.
  8|
  9|Deployed as a ConfigMap and mounted into the Wazuh Manager pod at:
 10|  /var/ossec/integrations/custom-w2thive.py
 11|
 12|Wazuh calls this script with:
 13|  python3 custom-w2thive.py <alert_file> <api_key> <hook_url>
 14|"""
 15|
 16|import json
 17|import logging
 18|import sys
 19|import os
 20|import requests
 21|from datetime import datetime
 22|
 23|# Configure logging to Wazuh integration log
 24|LOG_FILE = "/var/ossec/logs/integrations.log"
 25|logging.basicConfig(
 26|    filename=LOG_FILE if os.path.exists(os.path.dirname(LOG_FILE)) else None,
 27|    level=logging.INFO,
 28|    format="%(asctime)s %(levelname)s [w2thive] %(message)s",
 29|)
 30|log = logging.getLogger("w2thive")
 31|
 32|# Severity mapping: Wazuh alert level -> TheHive severity
 33|SEVERITY_MAP = {
 34|    range(1, 7): 1,    # Low
 35|    range(7, 10): 1,   # Low
 36|    range(10, 13): 2,  # Medium
 37|    range(13, 15): 3,  # High
 38|    range(15, 16): 4,  # Critical (Wazuh max is 15)
 39|}
 40|
 41|
 42|def get_severity(level: int) -> int:
 43|    """Map Wazuh alert level to TheHive severity."""
 44|    for level_range, severity in SEVERITY_MAP.items():
 45|        if level in level_range:
 46|            return severity
 47|    return 2  # Default: Medium
 48|
 49|
 50|def extract_observables(alert_data: dict) -> list:
 51|    """Extract observables (IOCs) from Wazuh alert JSON."""

52|    observables = []
 53|    data = alert_data.get("data", {})
 54|
 55|    # Source IP
 56|    if data.get("srcip"):
 57|        observables.append({
 58|            "dataType": "ip",
 59|            "data": data["srcip"],
 60|            "message": "Source IP from Wazuh alert",
 61|            "tags": ["wazuh", "source"]
 62|        })
 63|
 64|    # Destination IP
 65|    if data.get("dstip"):
 66|        observables.append({
 67|            "dataType": "ip",
 68|            "data": data["dstip"],
 69|            "message": "Destination IP from Wazuh alert",
 70|            "tags": ["wazuh", "destination"]
 71|        })
 72|
 73|    # URL
 74|    if data.get("url"):
 75|        observables.append({
 76|            "dataType": "url",
 77|            "data": data["url"],
 78|            "message": "URL from Wazuh alert",
 79|            "tags": ["wazuh"]
 80|        })
 81|
 82|    # File hashes
 83|    for hash_type in ["md5", "sha1", "sha256"]:
 84|        if data.get(hash_type):
 85|            observables.append({
 86|                "dataType": "hash",
 87|                "data": data[hash_type],
 88|                "message": f"{hash_type.upper()} hash from Wazuh alert",
 89|                "tags": ["wazuh", hash_type]
 90|            })
 91|
 92|    # Filename
 93|    if data.get("filename"):
 94|        observables.append({
 95|            "dataType": "filename",
 96|            "data": data["filename"],
 97|            "message": "Filename from Wazuh alert",
 98|            "tags": ["wazuh"]
 99|        })
100|
101|    # Username
102|    if data.get("srcuser"):
103|        observables.append({
104|            "dataType": "other",
105|            "data": data["srcuser"],
106|            "message": "Source username from Wazuh alert",
107|            "tags": ["wazuh", "username"]
108|        })
109|
110|    return observables
111|
112|
113|def create_thehive_alert(hook_url: str, api_key: str, alert: dict):
114|    """Create a TheHive alert from a Wazuh alert."""

115|    rule = alert.get("rule", {})
116|    agent = alert.get("agent", {})
117|    level = rule.get("level", 5)
118|
119|    # Build TheHive alert payload
120|    thehive_alert = {
121|        "title": f"[Wazuh] {rule.get('description', 'Unknown Alert')}",
122|        "description": (
123|            f"**Wazuh Alert (Level {level})**\n\n"
124|            f"- **Rule ID:** {rule.get('id', 'N/A')}\n"
125|            f"- **Rule Description:** {rule.get('description', 'N/A')}\n"
126|            f"- **Groups:** {', '.join(rule.get('groups', []))}\n"
127|            f"- **Agent:** {agent.get('name', 'N/A')} ({agent.get('id', 'N/A')})\n"
128|            f"- **Timestamp:** {alert.get('timestamp', 'N/A')}\n"
129|            f"- **Full Log:** {alert.get('full_log', 'N/A')[:500]}\n"
130|        ),
131|        "type": "wazuh-alert",
132|        "source": "Wazuh",
133|        "sourceRef": f"wazuh-{rule.get('id', '0')}-{alert.get('id', '0')}",
134|        "severity": get_severity(level),
135|        "tags": ["wazuh", f"level:{level}"] + rule.get("groups", []),
136|        "observables": extract_observables(alert),
137|    }
138|
139|    # MITRE ATT&CK tags if present
140|    mitre = rule.get("mitre", {})
141|    if mitre.get("id"):
142|        thehive_alert["tags"].extend(
143|            [f"mitre:{tid}" for tid in mitre["id"]]
144|        )
145|
146|    # Send to TheHive
147|    headers = {
148|        "Authorization": f"Bearer {api_key}",
149|        "Content-Type": "application/json"
150|    }
151|
152|    try:
153|        response = requests.post(
154|            f"{hook_url}/api/v1/alert",
155|            headers=headers,
156|            json=thehive_alert,
157|            timeout=10
158|        )
159|
160|        if response.status_code in (200, 201):
161|            alert_id = response.json().get("_id", "unknown")
162|            log.info(f"TheHive alert created: {alert_id} (rule {rule.get('id', '?')})")
163|        else:
164|            log.error(
165|                f"TheHive returned {response.status_code}: {response.text[:200]}"
166|            )
167|    except requests.exceptions.Timeout:
168|        log.error(f"TheHive request timed out: {hook_url}")
169|    except requests.exceptions.ConnectionError as e:
170|        log.error(f"Cannot reach TheHive at {hook_url}: {e}")
171|    except Exception as e:
172|        log.error(f"Unexpected error sending to TheHive: {e}")
173|
174|
175|def main():
176|    """Entry point — called by Wazuh integration framework."""
177|    if len(sys.argv) < 4:
178|        print("Usage: custom-w2thive.py <alert_file> <api_key> <hook_url>")
179|        sys.exit(1)
180|
181|    alert_file = sys.argv[1]
182|    api_key = sys.argv[2]
183|    hook_url = sys.argv[3]
184|
185|    try:
186|        with open(alert_file, "r") as f:
187|            alert = json.load(f)
188|    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
189|        log.error(f"Invalid JSON in alert file {alert_file}: {e}")
190|        sys.exit(1)
191|    except FileNotFoundError:
192|        log.error(f"Alert file not found: {alert_file}")
193|        sys.exit(1)
194|
195|    create_thehive_alert(hook_url, api_key, alert)
196|
197|
198|if __name__ == "__main__":
199|    main()
200|

1|# Wazuh Agent DaemonSet
 2|# Runs one agent per k3s node to monitor all containers and the host
 3|apiVersion: apps/v1
 4|kind: DaemonSet
 5|metadata:
 6|  name: wazuh-agent
 7|  namespace: wazuh
 8|  labels:
 9|    app: wazuh-agent
10|spec:
11|  selector:
12|    matchLabels:
13|      app: wazuh-agent
14|  template:
15|    metadata:
16|      labels:
17|        app: wazuh-agent
18|    spec:
19|      # Tolerate all taints to run on every node including control plane
20|      tolerations:
21|        - operator: Exists
22|      # Host networking for full visibility into node traffic
23|      hostNetwork: true
24|      hostPID: true
25|      containers:
26|        - name: wazuh-agent
27|          image: wazuh/wazuh-agent:4.14.3
28|          resources:
29|            requests:
30|              memory: "128Mi"
31|              cpu: "100m"
32|            limits:
33|              memory: "384Mi"
34|              cpu: "200m"
35|          env:
36|            - name: WAZUH_MANAGER
37|              value: "wazuh-manager.wazuh.svc.cluster.local"
38|            - name: WAZUH_AGENT_GROUP
39|              value: "k8s-nodes"
40|          # Mount host filesystem for FIM and log collection
41|          volumeMounts:
42|            - name: host-root
43|              mountPath: /host
44|              readOnly: true
45|            - name: host-var-log
46|              mountPath: /var/log
47|              readOnly: true
48|            - name: host-etc
49|              mountPath: /host/etc
50|              readOnly: true
51|          securityContext:
52|            privileged: true  # Required for rootcheck and FIM on host
53|      volumes:
54|        - name: host-root
55|          hostPath:
56|            path: /
57|        - name: host-var-log
58|          hostPath:
59|            path: /var/log
60|        - name: host-etc
61|          hostPath:
62|            path: /etc
63|

1|# Wazuh Dashboard — Deployment
  2|# OpenSearch Dashboards fork with Wazuh plugin preinstalled
  3|apiVersion: v1
  4|kind: Service
  5|metadata:
  6|  name: wazuh-dashboard
  7|  namespace: wazuh
  8|  labels:
  9|    app: wazuh-dashboard
 10|spec:
 11|  selector:
 12|    app: wazuh-dashboard
 13|  ports:
 14|    - name: https
 15|      port: 5601
 16|      targetPort: 5601
 17|---
 18|apiVersion: apps/v1
 19|kind: Deployment
 20|metadata:
 21|  name: wazuh-dashboard
 22|  namespace: wazuh
 23|  labels:
 24|    app: wazuh-dashboard
 25|spec:
 26|  replicas: 1
 27|  selector:
 28|    matchLabels:
 29|      app: wazuh-dashboard
 30|  template:
 31|    metadata:
 32|      labels:
 33|        app: wazuh-dashboard
 34|    spec:
 35|      automountServiceAccountToken: false
 36|      securityContext:
 37|        runAsNonRoot: true
 38|        runAsUser: 1000
 39|        fsGroup: 1000
 40|      containers:
 41|        - name: dashboard
 42|          image: wazuh/wazuh-dashboard:4.11.1
 43|          securityContext:
 44|            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
 45|            readOnlyRootFilesystem: false
 46|            capabilities:
 47|              drop: ["ALL"]
 48|          ports:
 49|            - containerPort: 5601
 50|              name: https
 51|          resources:
 52|            requests:
 53|              memory: "256Mi"
 54|              cpu: "100m"
 55|            limits:
 56|              memory: "512Mi"
 57|              cpu: "250m"
 58|          env:
 59|            - name: INDEXER_URL
 60|              value: "https://opensearch-cluster-master.shared.svc.cluster.local:9200"
 61|            - name: INDEXER_USERNAME
 62|              value: "admin"
 63|            - name: INDEXER_PASSWORD
 64|              valueFrom:
 65|                secretKeyRef:
 66|                  name: soc-shared-secrets
 67|                  key: opensearch-password
 68|                  optional: true
 69|            - name: WAZUH_API_URL
 70|              value: "https://wazuh-manager.wazuh.svc.cluster.

71|            - name: DASHBOARD_USERNAME
 72|              value: "kibanaserver"
 73|            - name: DASHBOARD_PASSWORD
 74|              valueFrom:
 75|                secretKeyRef:
 76|                  name: soc-shared-secrets
 77|                  key: opensearch-password
 78|                  optional: true
 79|            - name: API_USERNAME
 80|              value: "wazuh-wui"
 81|            - name: API_PASSWORD
 82|              valueFrom:
 83|                secretKeyRef:
 84|                  name: soc-wazuh-secrets
 85|                  key: wazuh-api-password
 86|                  optional: true
 87|          volumeMounts:
 88|            - name: certs
 89|              mountPath: /etc/wazuh-dashboard/certs
 90|              readOnly: true
 91|          readinessProbe:
 92|            httpGet:
 93|              port: 5601
 94|              path: /
 95|              scheme: HTTPS
 96|            initialDelaySeconds: 45
 97|            periodSeconds: 15
 98|            timeoutSeconds: 5
 99|          livenessProbe:
100|            httpGet:
101|              port: 5601
102|              path: /
103|              scheme: HTTPS
104|            initialDelaySeconds: 120
105|            periodSeconds: 30
106|            timeoutSeconds: 5
107|            failureThreshold: 6
108|      volumes:
109|        - name: certs
110|          secret:
111|            secretName: wazuh-dashboard-certs
112|

1|# Wazuh Helm Chart Values — SIEM/XDR Platform
 2|# Chart: wazuh/wazuh (community Helm chart)
 3|#
 4|# Note: Wazuh's official Helm chart is community-maintained. If unavailable,
 5|# deploy using the raw manifests in manager/, indexer/, dashboard/ directories.
 6|#
 7|# Install:
 8|#   helm install wazuh ./k8s/wazuh/ -n wazuh -f helm-values.yaml
 9|#   OR use individual manifests: kubectl apply -f k8s/wazuh/ -n wazuh
10|
11|# --- Wazuh Manager ---
12|manager:
13|  replicas: 1
14|  resources:
15|    requests:
16|      memory: "512Mi"
17|      cpu: "250m"
18|    limits:
19|      memory: "1Gi"
20|      cpu: "500m"
21|  # Use external OpenSearch (shared namespace)
22|  indexer:
23|    url: "https://opensearch.shared.svc.cluster.local:9200"
24|  # TheHive integration — forward high-severity alerts
25|  integration:
26|    thehive:
27|      enabled: true
28|      url: "http://thehive.thehive.svc.cluster.local:9000"
29|      # API key set via Kubernetes Secret
30|      alertLevel: 7  # Forward alerts at level 7 and above
31|  # Persistent storage for rules, decoders, and agent keys
32|  persistence:
33|    enabled: true
34|    size: "5Gi"
35|    storageClass: "local-path"
36|
37|# --- Wazuh Dashboard ---
38|dashboard:
39|  replicas: 1
40|  resources:
41|    requests:
42|      memory: "256Mi"
43|      cpu: "100m"
44|    limits:
45|      memory: "512Mi"
46|      cpu: "250m"
47|  # Point to shared OpenSearch
48|  opensearch:
49|    url: "https://opensearch.shared.svc.cluster.local:9200"
50|
51|# --- Wazuh Agent (DaemonSet) ---
52|agent:
53|  # DaemonSet runs one agent per k3s node
54|  resources:
55|    requests:
56|      memory: "128Mi"
57|      cpu: "100m"
58|    limits:
59|      memory: "384Mi"
60|      cpu: "200m"
61|  # Manager connection
62|  manager:
63|    host: "wazuh-manager.wazuh.svc.cluster.local"
64|    port: 1514
65|  # Monitoring modules
66|  modules:
67|    fileIntegrity: true       # FIM on critical paths
68|    rootkitDetection: true    # Rootcheck
69|    vulnerabilityScanner: true # CVE scanning
70|    logCollector: true         # Syslog collection
71|    securityConfigAssessment: true # CIS benchmarks
72|

1|# Custom Wazuh Log Decoders
 2|# Parse structured log formats from K8s and container runtimes
 3|apiVersion: v1
 4|kind: ConfigMap
 5|metadata:
 6|  name: wazuh-custom-decoders
 7|  namespace: wazuh
 8|  labels:
 9|    app: wazuh-manager
10|data:
11|  k8s-decoders.xml: |
12|    <!-- Kubernetes audit log decoder -->
13|    <decoder name="kubernetes-audit">
14|      <prematch>^{"kind":"Event","apiVersion":"audit.k8s.io</prematch>
15|    </decoder>
16|
17|    <decoder name="kubernetes-audit-fields">
18|      <parent>kubernetes-audit</parent>
19|      <regex type="json">
20|        verb=verb,
21|        user.username=user.username,
22|        objectRef.resource=objectRef.resource,
23|        objectRef.namespace=objectRef.namespace,
24|        objectRef.name=objectRef.name,
25|        responseStatus.code=responseStatus.code,
26|        sourceIPs=sourceIPs
27|      </regex>
28|    </decoder>
29|
30|    <!-- Container runtime (containerd) log decoder -->
31|    <decoder name="containerd-log">
32|      <prematch>^time="</prematch>
33|    </decoder>
34|
35|    <decoder name="containerd-log-fields">
36|      <parent>containerd-log</parent>
37|      <regex>^time="(\S+)" level=(\w+) msg="(.+)"</regex>
38|      <order>timestamp,severity,message</order>
39|    </decoder>
40|
41|    <!-- K3s server log decoder -->
42|    <decoder name="k3s-server">
43|      <prematch>^E\d{4}|^W\d{4}|^I\d{4}</prematch>
44|    </decoder>
45|
46|    <decoder name="k3s-server-fields">
47|      <parent>k3s-server</parent>
48|      <regex>^(\w)\d{4} (\S+)\s+\d+\s+(\S+):\d+\] (.+)</regex>
49|      <order>severity,timestamp,source_file,message</order>
50|    </decoder>
51|

1|# Wazuh Manager Configuration (ossec.conf)
  2|# Mounted as a ConfigMap into the Wazuh Manager pod
  3|apiVersion: v1
  4|kind: ConfigMap
  5|metadata:
  6|  name: wazuh-manager-config
  7|  namespace: wazuh
  8|  labels:
  9|    app: wazuh-manager
 10|data:
 11|  ossec.conf: |
 12|    <ossec_config>
 13|
 14|      <!-- Global settings -->
 15|      <global>
 16|        <jsonout_output>yes</jsonout_output>
 17|        <alerts_log>yes</alerts_log>
 18|        <logall>no</logall>
 19|        <logall_json>no</logall_json>
 20|      </global>
 21|
 22|      <!-- Agent enrollment -->
 23|      <auth>
 24|        <disabled>no</disabled>
 25|        <port>1515</port>
 26|        <use_source_ip>no</use_source_ip>
 27|        <purge>yes</purge>
 28|      </auth>
 29|
 30|      <!-- Remote agent connection -->
 31|      <remote>
 32|        <connection>secure</connection>
 33|        <port>1514</port>
 34|        <protocol>tcp</protocol>
 35|      </remote>
 36|
 37|      <!-- OpenSearch output — shared cluster in 'shared' namespace -->
 38|      <indexer>
 39|        <enabled>yes</enabled>
 40|        <hosts>
 41|          <host>https://opensearch.shared.svc.cluster.local:9200</host>
 42|        </hosts>
 43|        <!-- SSL disabled for home lab (internal ClusterIP only) -->
 44|      </indexer>
 45|
 46|      <!-- TheHive integration — forward alerts level 7+ to TheHive -->
 47|      <!-- This triggers the custom-w2thive.py script for each matching alert -->
 48|      <integration>
 49|        <name>custom-w2thive</name>
 50|        <hook_url>http://thehive.thehive.svc.cluster.local:9000</hook_url>
 51|        <api_key>THEHIVE_API_KEY_PLACEHOLDER</api_key>
 52|        <level>7</level>
 53|        <alert_format>json</alert_format>
 54|      </integration>
 55|
 56|      <!-- File Integrity Monitoring -->
 57|      <syscheck>
 58|        <disabled>no</disabled>
 59|        <frequency>43200</frequency> <!-- Check every 12 hours -->
 60|        <scan_on_start>yes</scan_on_start>
 61|        <!-- Critical paths to monitor -->
 62|        <directories check_all="yes" realtime="yes">/etc,/usr/bin,/usr/sbin</directories>
 63|        <directories check_all="yes" realtime="yes">/bin,/sbin</directories>
 64|        <!-- Ignore noisy paths -->
 65|        <ignore>/etc/mtab</ignore>
 66|        <ignore>/etc/hosts.

63|        <directories check_all="yes" realtime="yes">/bin,/sbin</directories>
 64|        <!-- Ignore noisy paths -->
 65|        <ignore>/etc/mtab</ignore>
 66|        <ignore>/etc/hosts.deny</ignore>
 67|        <ignore>/etc/mail/statistics</ignore>
 68|        <ignore>/etc/random-seed</ignore>
 69|        <ignore>/etc/adjtime</ignore>
 70|      </syscheck>
 71|
 72|      <!-- Rootkit detection -->
 73|      <rootcheck>
 74|        <disabled>no</disabled>
 75|        <check_files>yes</check_files>
 76|        <check_trojans>yes</check_trojans>
 77|        <check_dev>yes</check_dev>
 78|        <check_sys>yes</check_sys>
 79|        <check_pids>yes</check_pids>
 80|        <check_ports>yes</check_ports>
 81|        <check_if>yes</check_if>
 82|        <frequency>43200</frequency>
 83|      </rootcheck>
 84|
 85|      <!-- Vulnerability detection -->
 86|      <vulnerability-detection>
 87|        <enabled>yes</enabled>
 88|        <index-status>yes</index-status>
 89|        <feed-update-interval>60m</feed-update-interval>
 90|      </vulnerability-detection>
 91|
 92|      <!-- Security Configuration Assessment (CIS benchmarks) -->
 93|      <sca>
 94|        <enabled>yes</enabled>
 95|        <scan_on_start>yes</scan_on_start>
 96|        <interval>12h</interval>
 97|      </sca>
 98|
 99|      <!-- Log analysis — collect system logs -->
100|      <localfile>
101|        <log_format>syslog</log_format>
102|        <location>/var/log/syslog</location>
103|      </localfile>
104|      <localfile>
105|        <log_format>syslog</log_format>
106|        <location>/var/log/auth.log</location>
107|      </localfile>
108|
109|      <!-- Custom rules and decoders -->
110|      <ruleset>
111|        <decoder_dir>etc/decoders</decoder_dir>
112|        <rule_dir>etc/rules</rule_dir>
113|        <!-- Custom K8s rules -->
114|        <rule_dir>etc/rules/custom</rule_dir>
115|        <decoder_dir>etc/decoders/custom</decoder_dir>
116|      </ruleset>
117|
118|    </ossec_config>
119|

1|# Custom Wazuh Detection Rules
  2|# These rules supplement the built-in Wazuh ruleset with K8s-specific detections
  3|apiVersion: v1
  4|kind: ConfigMap
  5|metadata:
  6|  name: wazuh-custom-rules
  7|  namespace: wazuh
  8|  labels:
  9|    app: wazuh-manager
 10|data:
 11|  # Kubernetes-specific detection rules
 12|  k8s-rules.xml: |
 13|    <group name="kubernetes,">
 14|
 15|      <!-- K8s pod creation in sensitive namespace -->
 16|      <rule id="100100" level="7">
 17|        <decoded_as>json</decoded_as>
 18|        <field name="kubernetes.namespace_name">kube-system</field>
 19|        <field name="kubernetes.event.reason">Created</field>
 20|        <description>Pod created in kube-system namespace</description>
 21|        <group>kubernetes,pod_creation,</group>
 22|      </rule>
 23|
 24|      <!-- K8s failed authentication -->
 25|      <rule id="100101" level="10">
 26|        <decoded_as>json</decoded_as>
 27|        <field name="kubernetes.audit.responseStatus.code">^401$|^403$</field>
 28|        <description>Kubernetes API authentication/authorization failure</description>
 29|        <group>kubernetes,authentication_failure,</group>
 30|      </rule>
 31|
 32|      <!-- K8s privileged container -->
 33|      <rule id="100102" level="12">
 34|        <decoded_as>json</decoded_as>
 35|        <field name="kubernetes.pod.spec.containers.securityContext.privileged">true</field>
 36|        <description>Privileged container detected in Kubernetes</description>
 37|        <group>kubernetes,privilege_escalation,</group>
 38|      </rule>
 39|
 40|      <!-- K8s exec into pod (potential interactive shell) -->
 41|      <rule id="100103" level="8">
 42|        <decoded_as>json</decoded_as>
 43|        <field name="kubernetes.audit.verb">create</field>
 44|        <field name="kubernetes.audit.objectRef.subresource">exec</field>
 45|        <description>kubectl exec detected - interactive shell access to pod</description>
 46|        <group>kubernetes,exec,lateral_movement,</group>
 47|      </rule>
 48|
 49|      <!-- K8s secret access -->
 50|      <rule id="100104" level="7">
 51|        <decoded_as>json</decoded_as>
 52|        <field name="kubernetes.audit.verb">get|list</field>
 53|        <field name="kubernetes.audit.objectRef.resource">secrets</field>
 54|        <description>Kubernetes Secret accessed</description>
 55|        <group>kubernetes,secret_access,</group>
 56|      </rule>
 57|
 58|    </group>
 59|
 60|  # SSH brute force detection rules (enhanced)
 61|  ssh-rules.xml: |
 62|    <group name="sshd,custom_ssh,">
 63|
 64|      <!-- Multiple failed SSH login attempts from same source -->
 65|      <rule id="100200" level="10" frequency="5" timeframe="120">
 66|        <if_matched_sid>5716</if_matched_sid>
 67|        <same_source_ip />
 68|        <description>SSH brute force attack: 5+ failed logins in 2 minutes from $(srcip)</description>
 69|        <group>authentication_failures,brute_force,</group>
 70|      </rule>
 71|
 72|      <!-- Successful SSH login after multiple failures -->
 73|      <rule id="100201" level="12">
 74|        <if_matched_sid>100200</if_matched_sid>
 75|        <field name="srcip">\.

76|        <description>Possible SSH brute force success: login after multiple failures from $(srcip)</description>
 77|        <group>authentication_success,brute_force,</group>
 78|      </rule>
 79|
 80|    </group>
 81|
 82|  # Suspicious process detection
 83|  process-rules.xml: |
 84|    <group name="process,custom_process,">
 85|
 86|      <!-- Cryptocurrency miner detection -->
 87|      <rule id="100300" level="12">
 88|        <decoded_as>json</decoded_as>
 89|        <match>xmrig|minerd|cgminer|cpuminer|stratum+tcp</match>
 90|        <description>Possible cryptocurrency miner detected</description>
 91|        <group>malware,miner,</group>
 92|      </rule>
 93|
 94|      <!-- Reverse shell detection -->
 95|      <rule id="100301" level="14">
 96|        <decoded_as>json</decoded_as>
 97|        <match>bash -i >& /dev/tcp|nc -e /bin/sh|python -c.*socket.*connect</match>
 98|        <description>Possible reverse shell command detected</description>
 99|        <group>malware,reverse_shell,</group>
100|      </rule>
101|
102|      <!-- Suspicious download tools -->
103|      <rule id="100302" level="8">
104|        <decoded_as>json</decoded_as>
105|        <match>wget.*\|.*sh|curl.*\|.*bash|wget.*-O-.*\|</match>
106|        <description>Download and execute pattern detected</description>
107|        <group>suspicious_commands,download_exec,</group>
108|      </rule>
109|
110|    </group>
111|

1|# Wazuh Manager — StatefulSet deployment
  2|# SIEM/XDR core: receives agent logs, evaluates rules, triggers alerts
  3|apiVersion: v1
  4|kind: Service
  5|metadata:
  6|  name: wazuh-manager
  7|  namespace: wazuh
  8|  labels:
  9|    app: wazuh-manager
 10|spec:
 11|  selector:
 12|    app: wazuh-manager
 13|  ports:
 14|    - name: agent-events
 15|      port: 1514
 16|      targetPort: 1514
 17|    - name: agent-registration
 18|      port: 1515
 19|      targetPort: 1515
 20|    - name: api
 21|      port: 55000
 22|      targetPort: 55000
 23|  clusterIP: None
 24|---
 25|apiVersion: apps/v1
 26|kind: StatefulSet
 27|metadata:
 28|  name: wazuh-manager
 29|  namespace: wazuh
 30|  labels:
 31|    app: wazuh-manager
 32|spec:
 33|  serviceName: wazuh-manager
 34|  replicas: 1
 35|  selector:
 36|    matchLabels:
 37|      app: wazuh-manager
 38|  template:
 39|    metadata:
 40|      labels:
 41|        app: wazuh-manager
 42|    spec:
 43|      automountServiceAccountToken: false
 44|      containers:
 45|        - name: wazuh-manager
 46|          image: wazuh/wazuh-manager:4.14.3
 47|          securityContext:
 48|            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
 49|          ports:
 50|            - containerPort: 1514
 51|              name: agent-events
 52|            - containerPort: 1515
 53|              name: agent-reg
 54|            - containerPort: 55000
 55|              name: api
 56|          resources:
 57|            requests:
 58|              memory: "512Mi"
 59|              cpu: "250m"
 60|            limits:
 61|              memory: "1Gi"
 62|              cpu: "500m"
 63|          env:
 64|            - name: INDEXER_URL
 65|              value: "https://opensearch-cluster-master.shared.svc.cluster.local:9200"
 66|            - name: INDEXER_USERNAME
 67|              value: "admin"
 68|            - name: INDEXER_PASSWORD
 69|              valueFrom:
 70|                secretKeyRef:
 71|                  name: soc-shared-secrets
 72|                  key: opensearch-password
 73|                  optional: true
 74|            - name: FILEBEAT_SSL_VERIFICATION_MODE
 75|              value: "none"
 76|          volumeMounts:
 77|            - name: data
 78|              mountPath: /var/ossec/data
 79|            - name: config
 80|              mountPath: /var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf
 81|              subPath: ossec.

81|              subPath: ossec.conf
 82|            - name: custom-rules
 83|              mountPath: /var/ossec/etc/rules/custom
 84|            - name: custom-decoders
 85|              mountPath: /var/ossec/etc/decoders/custom
 86|          readinessProbe:
 87|            tcpSocket:
 88|              port: 1514
 89|            initialDelaySeconds: 45
 90|            periodSeconds: 15
 91|            timeoutSeconds: 5
 92|          livenessProbe:
 93|            tcpSocket:
 94|              port: 1514
 95|            initialDelaySeconds: 120
 96|            periodSeconds: 30
 97|            timeoutSeconds: 5
 98|            failureThreshold: 6
 99|      volumes:
100|        - name: config
101|          configMap:
102|            name: wazuh-manager-config
103|        - name: custom-rules
104|          configMap:
105|            name: wazuh-custom-rules
106|        - name: custom-decoders
107|          configMap:
108|            name: wazuh-custom-decoders
109|  volumeClaimTemplates:
110|    - metadata:
111|        name: data
112|      spec:
113|        accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
114|        storageClassName: "local-path"
115|        resources:
116|          requests:
117|            storage: 5Gi
118|

1|# Lima VM Definition for MatchBox
  2|# Runs k3s Kubernetes inside a lightweight Linux VM on macOS (Apple Silicon)
  3|#
  4|# Usage:
  5|#   limactl start ./lima/k3s-soc.yaml
  6|#   export KUBECONFIG=$(limactl list k3s-soc --format '{{.Dir}}/copied-from-guest/kubeconfig.yaml')
  7|#   kubectl get nodes
  8|
  9|# VM image — Ubuntu 24.04 LTS (ARM64 for Apple Silicon)
 10|images:
 11|  - location: "https://cloud-images.ubuntu.com/releases/24.04/release/ubuntu-24.04-server-cloudimg-arm64.img"
 12|    arch: "aarch64"
 13|
 14|# Resource allocation — slim config for Mac Mini M4 (16GB total)
 15|# macOS needs ~4-5GB, Lima overhead ~500MB, leaves ~10GB for the VM
 16|cpus: 4
 17|memory: "10GiB"
 18|disk: "120GiB"
 19|
 20|# VZ framework — Apple's native virtualization, near-native performance on M-series
 21|vmType: "vz"
 22|# Rosetta 2 — enables running x86_64 container images on ARM transparently
 23|rosetta:
 24|  enabled: true
 25|  binfmt: true
 26|
 27|# Mount the k8s manifests directory into the VM for easy access
 28|mounts:
 29|  - location: "~/MatchBox"
 30|    mountPoint: "/home/{{.User}}/SecurityCenter"
 31|    writable: false
 32|
 33|# Port forwarding — expose k3s API and service ports to macOS host
 34|portForwards:
 35|  # k3s API server — for kubectl from macOS
 36|  - guestPort: 6443
 37|    hostPort: 6443
 38|    proto: tcp
 39|  # HTTP ingress (Traefik)
 40|  - guestPort: 80
 41|    hostPort: 80
 42|    proto: tcp
 43|  # HTTPS ingress (Traefik)
 44|  - guestPort: 443
 45|    hostPort: 443
 46|    proto: tcp
 47|  # Wazuh agent communication — for monitoring external hosts on LAN
 48|  - guestPort: 1514
 49|    hostPort: 1514
 50|    proto: tcp
 51|  # Wazuh agent registration
 52|  - guestPort: 1515
 53|    hostPort: 1515
 54|    proto: tcp
 55|  # Wazuh API (for MCP server on macOS)
 56|  - guestPort: 55000
 57|    hostPort: 55000
 58|    proto: tcp
 59|
 60|# Provisioning script — runs on first boot
 61|provision:
 62|  - mode: system
 63|    script: |
 64|      #!/bin/bash
 65|      set -eux
 66|
 67|      # --- Swap Configuration ---
 68|      # 4GB swap as safety net for memory pressure situations
 69|      if [ !

70|        fallocate -l 4G /swapfile
 71|        chmod 600 /swapfile
 72|        mkswap /swapfile
 73|        swapon /swapfile
 74|        echo '/swapfile none swap sw 0 0' >> /etc/fstab
 75|      fi
 76|
 77|      # --- System Tuning ---
 78|      # Increase vm.max_map_count for OpenSearch (requires 262144+)
 79|      echo "vm.max_map_count=262144" >> /etc/sysctl.d/99-opensearch.conf
 80|      sysctl -w vm.max_map_count=262144
 81|
 82|      # Increase file descriptor limits for OpenSearch and Wazuh
 83|      echo "* soft nofile 65536" >> /etc/security/limits.conf
 84|      echo "* hard nofile 65536" >> /etc/security/limits.conf
 85|
 86|      # --- Install k3s ---
 87|      # --disable=traefik: We'll install our own Traefik with custom config
 88|      # --write-kubeconfig-mode=644: Allows non-root kubeconfig access
 89|      # --kubelet-arg: Set system-reserved to protect the node from OOM
 90|      curl -sfL https://get.k3s.io | INSTALL_K3S_EXEC="server \
 91|        --disable=traefik \
 92|        --write-kubeconfig-mode=644 \
 93|        --kubelet-arg=system-reserved=cpu=200m,memory=256Mi \
 94|        --kubelet-arg=eviction-hard=memory.available<200Mi \
 95|        --kubelet-arg=eviction-soft=memory.available<500Mi \
 96|        --kubelet-arg=eviction-soft-grace-period=memory.available=30s" \
 97|        sh -
 98|
 99|      # --- Install Helm ---
100|      curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/helm/helm/main/scripts/get-helm-3 | bash
101|
102|      # --- Export kubeconfig for Lima host access ---
103|      mkdir -p /home/{{.User}}/.kube
104|      cp /etc/rancher/k3s/k3s.yaml /home/{{.User}}/.kube/config
105|      # Replace 127.0.0.1 with 0.0.0.0 so it works from macOS via port forward
106|      sed -i 's/127.0.0.1/0.0.0.0/g' /home/{{.User}}/.kube/config
107|      chown -R $(id -u {{.User}}):$(id -g {{.User}}) /home/{{.User}}/.kube
108|
109|      # --- Install NFS server packages (for Cortex shared filesystem) ---
110|      apt-get update && apt-get install -y nfs-kernel-server
111|
112|      # --- Wait for k3s to be ready ---
113|      until kubectl --kubeconfig=/etc/rancher/k3s/k3s.yaml get nodes 2>/dev/null; do
114|        echo "Waiting for k3s..."
115|        sleep 5
116|      done
117|      echo "k3s is ready!"
118|
119|# Copy kubeconfig to host for kubectl access from macOS
120|copyToHost:
121|  - guest: "/etc/rancher/k3s/k3s.yaml"
122|    host: "{{.Dir}}/copied-from-guest/kubeconfig.yaml"
123|
124|# DNS — resolve *.soc.homelab.local inside the VM
125|dns:
126|  - hosts:
127|      soc.homelab.local: "127.0.0.1"
128|
